##
## Dev Environment - Main Configuration
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
##
## Purpose: Orchestrates all infrastructure modules for the development environment
## Environment: Development (cost-optimized, single-region)
## VNet: 10.0.0.0/16
## Subnets: database (10.0.2.0/24), container-apps (10.0.3.0/24), legacy-apps (10.0.4.0/24)
##
## Modules & Resources Deployed:
## - Networking: VNet, subnets, NSGs
## - Storage: Storage accounts, Key Vault, Log Analytics
## - PostgreSQL: Flexible Server (Burstable B2s, 50GB Premium SSD) in database subnet
##


##=============================================================================
## CURRENT AZURE CONTEXT
##=============================================================================
## Get current subscription and tenant information for identity and RBAC configuration

data "azurerm_client_config" "current" {}



##=============================================================================
## RESOURCE GROUP
##=============================================================================
## Primary container for all development environment resources
## Resource naming convention: rg-intellxxdna-lexxi-dev
## Tags are managed by IaC and ignore manual changes

# Create Resource Group
resource "azurerm_resource_group" "rg" {

  name     = local.resource_group_name
  location = var.azure_region
  tags     = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [tags]
  }
}



##=============================================================================
## NETWORKING MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Creates Virtual Network and subnets for the development environment
## Configuration:
## - VNet: 10.0.0.0/16
## - Container-Apps subnet: 10.0.3.0/24 (for container deployments)
## - Legacy-Apps subnet: 10.0.4.0/24 (for traditional VMs/apps)
## 
## Each subnet includes:
## - Network Security Group (NSG) for traffic control
## - Service endpoints for Azure services (SQL, Storage, Key Vault)
## - Default allow rule for internal VNet communication

# Networking Module
module "networking" {

  source = "../../modules/networking"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix

  vnet_address_space = var.vnet_address_space

  subnets = {

    database = {
      address_prefix = "10.0.2.0/24"
      description    = "Database private subnet"
    }
    container-apps = {
      address_prefix = "10.0.3.0/24"
      description    = "Container Apps private subnet"
    }
    legacy-apps = {
      address_prefix = "10.0.4.0/24"
      description    = "Legacy applications private subnet"
    }
    bastion = {
      address_prefix = "10.0.5.0/24"
      description    = "Bastion jump host subnet"
    }
  }

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [azurerm_resource_group.rg]
}



##=============================================================================
## STORAGE MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Manages storage infrastructure and monitoring
## Resources created:
## - Storage Account: General-purpose v2, LRS replication
## - Key Vault: Secure credential storage
## - Log Analytics Workspace: Central logging and monitoring
##
## Key Vault contains:
## - Secrets for database credentials (future use)
## - API keys and sensitive configuration
## - Connection strings and access tokens
##
## Log Analytics stores:
## - Resource diagnostics and metrics
## - Application logs (when configured)
##
## Backup Settings:
## - Enabled: false (cost optimization for dev)
## - Retention: 7 days (minimal for development)

# Storage Module
module "storage" {

  source = "../../modules/storage"

  resource_group_name = module.networking.resource_group_name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix

  enable_backup         = local.env_config.enable_backup
  backup_retention_days = local.env_config.backup_retention
  log_retention_days    = local.env_config.log_retention_days

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [module.networking]
}



##=============================================================================
## REDIS CACHE MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Azure Cache for Redis for distributed caching
## Configuration:
## - Tier: Basic (most cost-effective)
## - Capacity: C0 (250MB minimum)
## - No replication (single node)
## - Non-SSL port enabled for dev convenience
## - Credentials stored securely in Key Vault
##
## Use cases:
## - Session caching
## - Query result caching
## - Rate limiting
## - Real-time data caching

# module "redis" {
# 
#   source = "../../modules/redis"
# 
#   resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
#   location            = var.azure_region
#   resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
# 
#   sku_name      = "Basic"
#   family        = "C"
#   capacity      = 0 # 250MB - minimum for dev
#   redis_version = "6"
# 
#   # Private network configuration - accessible only from VNet
#   public_network_access_enabled = false # Disable public access - private only
#   enable_non_ssl_port           = false # SSL only for secure communication
# 
#   # Allow traffic only from container-apps and legacy-apps subnets (same as PostgreSQL)
#   allowed_cidr_blocks = [
#     {
#       name     = "AllowContainerAppsSubnet"
#       start_ip = "10.0.3.0"
#       end_ip   = "10.0.3.255"
#     },
#     {
#       name     = "AllowLegacyAppsSubnet"
#       start_ip = "10.0.4.0"
#       end_ip   = "10.0.4.255"
#     }
#   ]
# 
#   key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
#   tags         = local.tags
# 
#   depends_on = [module.storage]
# }



##=============================================================================
## POSTGRESQL DATABASE MODULE
##=============================================================================
## COMMENTED OUT - Cost Optimization (Re-enable in 2-3 weeks as needed)
## PostgreSQL Flexible Server for development environment will be re-enabled when needed
## To enable: Uncomment resources below and PostgreSQL env vars in Container App
##=============================================================================
## POSTGRESQL DATABASE MODULE
##=============================================================================
## PostgreSQL Flexible Server for development environment
## Re-enabled for Keycloak deployment
## Databases: intellxxdna_dev (main app) + keycloak (Keycloak identity service)
##

# PostgreSQL Flexible Server
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server" "postgres" {

  name                = "${local.resource_prefix}-psql"
  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region

  # Deployment
  version                = "14"
  administrator_login    = var.postgres_admin_username
  administrator_password = var.postgres_admin_password

  # Compute & Storage
  sku_name   = "B_Standard_B2s"
  storage_mb = 65536 # 64 GiB in MB

  # Backup
  backup_retention_days        = 7
  geo_redundant_backup_enabled = false

  # Network
  delegated_subnet_id           = module.networking.database_subnet_id
  private_dns_zone_id           = azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres.id
  public_network_access_enabled = false

  auto_grow_enabled = false

  tags = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [zone]
  }

  depends_on = [azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres,
  azurerm_private_dns_zone_virtual_network_link.postgres]
}

# Private DNS Zone for PostgreSQL
resource "azurerm_private_dns_zone" "postgres" {

  name                = "dev.postgres.database.azure.com"
  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name

  tags = local.tags
}

# Link Private DNS Zone to VNet
resource "azurerm_private_dns_zone_virtual_network_link" "postgres" {

  name                  = "${local.resource_prefix}-postgres-vnet-link"
  resource_group_name   = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  private_dns_zone_name = azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres.name
  virtual_network_id    = module.networking.vnet_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_private_dns_zone.postgres]
}

# Database for the application
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_database" "app_database" {

  name      = "intellxxdna"
  server_id = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  charset   = "UTF8"
  collation = "en_US.utf8"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# Database for Keycloak
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_database" "keycloak_database" {

  name      = "keycloak"
  server_id = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  charset   = "UTF8"
  collation = "en_US.utf8"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}


##=============================================================================
## KEY VAULT SECRETS - DATABASE CREDENTIALS
##=============================================================================
## PostgreSQL connection parameters stored securely in Key Vault
## Re-enabled for Keycloak and application database access
##=============================================================================

# PostgreSQL Host
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_host" {
  name         = "postgres-host"
  value        = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.fqdn
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# PostgreSQL Port
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_port" {
  name         = "postgres-port"
  value        = "5432"
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# PostgreSQL Database Name
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_postgres_db" {
  name         = "lexxi-postgres-db"
  value        = "intellxxdna"
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# PostgreSQL Admin Username
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_user" {
  name         = "postgres-user"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_username
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# PostgreSQL Admin Password
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "postgres_password" {
  name         = "postgres-password"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_password
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# RD PostgreSQL URL (Full connection string)
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "intellxxdna_postgres_url" {
  name         = "rd-postgres-url"
  value        = "postgresql+asyncpg://${var.intellxxdna_admin_username}:${urlencode(var.intellxxdna_admin_password)}@${azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.fqdn}:5432/intellxxdna"
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# Disable SSL requirement
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_configuration" "require_secure_transport" {
  name      = "require_secure_transport"
  server_id = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  value     = "off"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# PostgreSQL Firewall Rule - Allow container-apps subnet
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_firewall_rule" "container_apps" {

  name             = "AllowContainerAppsSubnet"
  server_id        = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  start_ip_address = "10.0.3.0"
  end_ip_address   = "10.0.3.255"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# PostgreSQL Firewall Rule - Allow legacy-apps subnet
resource "azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_firewall_rule" "legacy_apps" {

  name             = "AllowLegacyAppsSubnet"
  server_id        = azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.id
  start_ip_address = "10.0.4.0"
  end_ip_address   = "10.0.4.255"

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}



##=============================================================================
## CONTAINER APPS ENVIRONMENT & APPLICATION
##=============================================================================
## Azure Container Apps for containerized workloads
## Configuration:
## - Plan: Consumption (pay-per-use, auto-scaling)
## - Environment: Connected to container-apps subnet (10.0.3.0/24)
## - Image: lexxi from shared ACR (ixdnashared.azurecr.io)
## - Resources: 1 vCPU, 2GB memory
## - Replicas: Minimum 1, Maximum 3
##
## The Container Apps Environment integrates with the VNet for
## secure, isolated container deployments within the private network

# Container Apps Environment connected to VNet
resource "azurerm_container_app_environment" "env" {

  name                           = "${local.resource_prefix}-cae"
  location                       = var.azure_region
  resource_group_name            = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  log_analytics_workspace_id     = module.storage.log_analytics_workspace_id
  infrastructure_subnet_id       = module.networking.container_apps_subnet_id
  internal_load_balancer_enabled = false

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [module.networking, module.storage]

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [tags, infrastructure_resource_group_name]
  }
}



#
# Container App - RD
#

# RD Swagger user name.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_swagger_username" {
  name  = "rd-swagger-username"
  value = var.rd_swagger_username

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD Swagger user password.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_swagger_pw" {
  name  = "rd-swagger-pw"
  value = var.rd_swagger_pw

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD environment.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_environment" {
  name  = "rd-environment"
  value = var.rd_environment

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD JWT_SECRET_KEY.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_jwt_secret_key" {
  name  = "rd-jwt-secret-key"
  value = var.rd_jwt_secret_key

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_cors_allow_origins" {
  name  = "rd-cors-allow-origins"
  value = var.rd_cors_allow_origins

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_access_token_expire_minutes" {
  name  = "rd-access-token-expire-minutes"
  value = var.rd_access_token_expire_minutes

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD Sentry DSN.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "rd_sentry_dsn" {
  name  = "rd-sentry-dsn"
  value = var.rd_sentry_dsn

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# RD Container app.
resource "azurerm_container_app" "rd" {

  name                         = "${local.resource_prefix}-rd"
  container_app_environment_id = azurerm_container_app_environment.env.id
  resource_group_name          = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  revision_mode                = "Single"

  # Enable managed identity for Key Vault secret access
  identity {
    type = "SystemAssigned"
  }

  # Enable ingress for public access
  ingress {

    allow_insecure_connections = true
    external_enabled           = true
    target_port                = 3000
    transport                  = "http"

    dynamic "ip_security_restriction" {
      for_each = { for idx, ip in local.front_door_backend_ips : "fd${idx}" => ip }
      content {
        name             = ip_security_restriction.key
        action           = "Allow"
        ip_address_range = ip_security_restriction.value
        description      = "Azure Front Door backend IP"
      }
    }

    traffic_weight {
      latest_revision = true
      percentage      = 100
    }
  }

  template {

    container {

      name   = "rd"
      image  = "${var.shared_container_registry_login_server}/${var.container_rd_app_image_name}:dev-latest"
      cpu    = var.container_app_cpu
      memory = var.container_app_memory

      # Database credentials from Key Vault
      # Note: Container Apps doesn't support @Microsoft.KeyVault() syntax
      # We fetch actual values from Key Vault via Terraform at apply time
      env {
        name  = "POSTGRES_URL"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.intellxxdna_postgres_url.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SWAGGER_USERNAME"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_swagger_username.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SWAGGER_PASSWORD"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_swagger_pw.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "ENVIRONMENT"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_environment.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "JWT_SECRET_KEY"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_jwt_secret_key.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_cors_allow_origins.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_access_token_expire_minutes.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SENTRY_DSN"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.rd_sentry_dsn.value
      }
    }

    min_replicas = var.container_app_min_replicas
    max_replicas = var.container_app_max_replicas
  }

  registry {
    server               = var.shared_container_registry_login_server
    username             = var.shared_container_registry_username
    password_secret_name = "acr-password"
  }

  secret {
    name  = "acr-password"
    value = var.shared_container_registry_password
  }

  tags = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [workload_profile_name]
  }

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app_environment.env,
    azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.intellxxdna_postgres_url
    # module.redis
  ]
}



#
# Container App - Lexxi
#

# Lexxi OpenAI token
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_openai_token" {
  name  = "lexxi-openai-token"
  value = var.lexxi_openai_token

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# Lexxi CORS allow origins
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_cors_allow_origins" {
  name  = "lexxi-cors-allow-origins"
  value = var.lexxi_cors_allow_origins

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# Lexxi Sentry DSN.
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "lexxi_sentry_dsn" {
  name  = "lexxi-sentry-dsn"
  value = var.lexxi_sentry_dsn

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [module.storage]
}

# Lexxi Container app.
resource "azurerm_container_app" "lexxi" {

  name                         = "${local.resource_prefix}-lexxi"
  container_app_environment_id = azurerm_container_app_environment.env.id
  resource_group_name          = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  revision_mode                = "Single"

  # Enable managed identity for Key Vault secret access
  identity {
    type = "SystemAssigned"
  }

  # Enable ingress for public access
  ingress {

    allow_insecure_connections = true
    external_enabled           = true
    target_port                = 3000
    transport                  = "http"

    dynamic "ip_security_restriction" {
      for_each = { for idx, ip in local.front_door_backend_ips : "fd${idx}" => ip }
      content {
        name             = ip_security_restriction.key
        action           = "Allow"
        ip_address_range = ip_security_restriction.value
        description      = "Azure Front Door backend IP"
      }
    }

    traffic_weight {
      latest_revision = true
      percentage      = 100
    }
  }

  template {

    container {

      name   = "lexxi"
      image  = "${var.shared_container_registry_login_server}/${var.container_lexxi_app_image_name}:dev-latest"
      cpu    = var.container_app_cpu
      memory = var.container_app_memory

      # Database credentials from Key Vault
      # Note: Container Apps doesn't support @Microsoft.KeyVault() syntax
      # We fetch actual values from Key Vault via Terraform at apply time
      env {
        name  = "POSTGRES_URL"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.intellxxdna_postgres_url.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "OPENAI_API_KEY"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_openai_token.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_cors_allow_origins.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "SENTRY_DSN"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_sentry_dsn.value
      }

      # Redis configuration from module
      # Note: Redis secrets are already stored in Key Vault by the redis module
      # We reference the module outputs here which come from the Redis resource
      # env {
      #   name  = "REDIS_HOST"
      #   value = module.redis.redis_hostname
      # }
      # 
      # env {
      #   name  = "REDIS_PORT"
      #   value = tostring(module.redis.redis_port)
      # }
      # 
      # env {
      #   name  = "REDIS_PASSWORD"
      #   value = module.redis.redis_primary_access_key
      # }
      # 
      # env {
      #   name  = "REDIS_CONNECTION_STRING"
      #   value = module.redis.redis_primary_connection_string
      # }
    }

    min_replicas = var.container_app_min_replicas
    max_replicas = var.container_app_max_replicas
  }

  registry {
    server               = var.shared_container_registry_login_server
    username             = var.shared_container_registry_username
    password_secret_name = "acr-password"
  }

  secret {
    name  = "acr-password"
    value = var.shared_container_registry_password
  }

  # NOTE: Lexxi container app uses Terraform-fetched values from Key Vault
  # (see env blocks above), so no additional secret blocks needed here

  tags = local.tags

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [workload_profile_name]
  }

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app_environment.env,
    azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_host,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_port,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.lexxi_postgres_db,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_user,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.postgres_password
    # module.redis
  ]
}






##=============================================================================
## KEYCLOAK CONTAINER APP
##=============================================================================
## Keycloak Identity and Access Management (IAM)
## Configuration:
## - Image: keycloak/keycloak:latest (official Docker image)
## - Database: PostgreSQL database "keycloak"
## - Admin: Configured via environment variables
## - Features: OIDC/OAuth2, LDAP/AD integration, realms, clients
## - Access: Public via Front Door + direct
## - Port: 8080
##

##=============================================================================
## KEY VAULT SECRETS - KEYCLOAK CONFIGURATION
##=============================================================================
## Keycloak-specific configuration stored securely in Key Vault
## These values are referenced by the Keycloak container app via Key Vault URIs
##=============================================================================

# Keycloak Database URL (JDBC connection string)
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_db_url" {
  name  = "keycloak-db-url"
  value = "jdbc:postgresql://${azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres.fqdn}/keycloak"

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id

  depends_on = [azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server.postgres]
}

# Keycloak Database Username
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_db_username" {
  name         = "keycloak-db-username"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_username
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Database Password
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_db_password" {
  name         = "keycloak-db-password"
  value        = var.postgres_admin_password
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Admin Username
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_admin" {
  name         = "keycloak-admin"
  value        = var.keycloak_admin_username
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Admin Password
resource "azurerm_key_vault_secret" "keycloak_admin_password" {
  name         = "keycloak-admin-password"
  value        = var.keycloak_admin_password
  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
}

# Keycloak Container App
resource "azurerm_container_app" "keycloak" {

  name                         = "${local.resource_prefix}-auth"
  container_app_environment_id = azurerm_container_app_environment.env.id
  resource_group_name          = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  revision_mode                = "Single"

  # Enable managed identity for Key Vault secret access
  identity {
    type = "SystemAssigned"
  }

  # Enable ingress for public access
  # Note: Multiple revisions allow zero-downtime deployments with traffic splitting
  # GitHub Actions will manage gradual traffic shift from old revision (90%) to new revision (10%)
  ingress {

    allow_insecure_connections = true
    external_enabled           = true
    target_port                = 8080
    transport                  = "http"

    dynamic "ip_security_restriction" {
      for_each = { for idx, ip in local.front_door_backend_ips : "fd${idx}" => ip }
      content {
        name             = ip_security_restriction.key
        action           = "Allow"
        ip_address_range = ip_security_restriction.value
        description      = "Azure Front Door backend IP"
      }
    }

    # B.1 Zero-Downtime Deployment Strategy with Traffic Splitting
    # Allows gradual rollout: 10% new, 90% old → 25/75 → 50/50 → 75/25 → 100% new
    # Deployment script (bin/deploy-keycloak-zero-downtime.sh) dynamically manages traffic weights
    # and updates revision references as new revisions are created by Azure

    traffic_weight {
      latest_revision = true
      percentage      = 100 # Terraform baseline: 100% on latest
      # Deployment script modifies this to 10% for zero-downtime rollout
    }
  }

  template {

    container {

      name   = "keycloak"
      image  = "keycloak/keycloak:latest"
      cpu    = 1.0
      memory = "2Gi"

      # Start Keycloak in production mode
      command = ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh", "start"]

      # Database configuration - values from Key Vault secrets
      # Note: References the secret block definitions below for Key Vault integration

      # Database type.
      env {
        name  = "KC_DB"
        value = "postgres"
      }

      # Database url, username, and password from Key Vault secrets
      env {
        name  = "KC_DB_URL"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_url.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_DB_USERNAME"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_username.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_DB_PASSWORD"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_password.value
      }

      # Admin credentials - values from Key Vault secrets

      env {
        name  = "KEYCLOAK_ADMIN"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin.value
      }

      env {
        name  = "KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD"
        value = azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin_password.value
      }

      # Note: Keycloak has strict type validation for configuration parameters,
      # so boolean and enum values must be direct values, not Key Vault references

      env {
        name  = "KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT"
        value = "false"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_HTTP_ENABLED"
        value = "true"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_PROXY"
        value = "edge"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_PROXY_HEADERS"
        value = "xforwarded"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_SCHEME"
        value = "https"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_HTTP_RELATIVE_PATH"
        value = "/auth"
      }

      env {
        name  = "KC_SSL_REQUIRED"
        value = "none"
      }
    }

    min_replicas = 1
    max_replicas = 2
  }

  tags = local.tags

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app_environment.env,
    azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_database.keycloak_database,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_url,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_username,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_db_password,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin,
    azurerm_key_vault_secret.keycloak_admin_password
  ]
}

##=============================================================================
## KEY VAULT ACCESS POLICY - KEYCLOAK
##=============================================================================
## Grant Keycloak container app managed identity permissions to read secrets
## from Key Vault for environment variable substitution
## NOTE: This resource is commented out initially and must be applied in a 
## second step after the Keycloak container app (and its identity) is created.
## To apply: Uncomment this block and run terraform apply again.
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_key_vault_access_policy" "keycloak" {

  key_vault_id = module.storage.key_vault_id
  tenant_id    = data.azurerm_client_config.current.tenant_id
  object_id    = azurerm_container_app.keycloak.identity[0].principal_id

  secret_permissions = ["Get", "List"]

  depends_on = [azurerm_container_app.keycloak]
}



##=============================================================================
## APPLICATION GATEWAY MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Public gateway with WAF for secure access to Container App
## Configuration:
## - SKU: Standard_v2 (cost-optimized)
## - WAF: Enabled in Detection mode (logs threats, doesn't block)
## - Listener: HTTP -> HTTPS redirect
## - Backend: Container App (internal FQDN)
## - Certificate: Self-signed for development
##
## Access:
## - Public IP: <ip_address>
## - URL: https://<ip_address> (self-signed cert warning expected)
## - HTTP automatically redirects to HTTPS

##=============================================================================
## AZURE FRONT DOOR MODULE
##=============================================================================
## Provides global public access with managed HTTPS (*.azurefd.net certificate)
## Features:
## - Automatic HTTPS certificate management (no renewal needed)
## - WAF protection with OWASP ModSecurity rules
## - Global CDN with DDoS protection (layers 3-7)
## - HTTP → HTTPS automatic redirect
## - Health probes for backend monitoring
## Status: Replaces Application Gateway for lower cost ($6/month vs $32/month) and simplicity
##

module "front_door" {

  source = "../../modules/front-door"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  tags                = local.tags

  # Front Door Configuration
  front_door_sku          = var.front_door_sku
  custom_domain_host_name = "api-dev.ixxdna.com"

  # Backend Configuration - Multiple backends for Lexxi, RD, and Keycloak with path-based routing
  # NOTE: Route Keycloak over HTTPS to bypass HTTP-only proxy header issues
  backends = [
    {
      name              = "lexxi"
      fqdn              = azurerm_container_app.lexxi.ingress[0].fqdn
      port              = 80
      protocol          = "Http"
      path              = "/lexxi/*"
      health_path       = "/"
      strip_path_prefix = true
    },
    {
      name              = "rd"
      fqdn              = azurerm_container_app.rd.ingress[0].fqdn
      port              = 80
      protocol          = "Http"
      path              = "/rd/*"
      health_path       = "/"
      strip_path_prefix = true
    },
    {
      name             = "keycloak"
      fqdn             = azurerm_container_app.keycloak.ingress[0].fqdn
      port             = 443
      protocol         = "Https"
      path             = "/auth/*"
      health_path      = "/auth/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration"
      session_affinity = true
    }
  ]

  # WAF Configuration
  enable_waf = var.enable_front_door_waf
  waf_mode   = var.front_door_waf_mode

  depends_on = [
    azurerm_container_app.lexxi,
    azurerm_container_app.rd,
    azurerm_container_app.keycloak,
    module.networking
  ]
}



##=============================================================================
## STATIC WEB APP MODULE (lexxi)
##=============================================================================
## Azure Static Web App for Angular frontend deployment
## Features:
## - Free tier (no cost)
## - GitHub Actions CI/CD integration
## - Managed HTTPS certificate (*.azurestaticapps.net)
## - Global CDN distribution
## - API proxy to backend via staticwebapp.config.json
## 
## Deployment:
## - Automatically deployed from develop branch
## - Build command: ng build
## - Build output: dist/
## - GitHub Enterprise authentication via PAT token
##
## Access:
## - Frontend: https://<app-name>.azurestaticapps.net
## - API: Proxied through Static Web App to Front Door endpoint
##

module "static_web_app_lexxi" {

  source = "../../modules/static-web-app"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  app_name            = "lexxis"

  # GitHub Configuration
  github_repo_url = var.lexxi_github_repo_url
  github_token    = var.github_token
  github_branch   = var.lexxi_github_branch

  # Build Configuration
  app_location    = var.swa_app_location
  output_location = var.swa_output_location
  build_command   = var.swa_build_command

  # API Configuration
  api_endpoint = module.front_door.endpoint_fqdn
  api_key      = ""

  sku_tier = "Free"
  sku_size = "Free"
  tags     = local.tags

  depends_on = [
    module.front_door,
    azurerm_container_app.lexxi
  ]
}

## STATIC WEB APP CUSTOM DOMAIN for lexxi
resource "azurerm_static_web_app_custom_domain" "lexxi_dev" {

  count             = var.enable_custom_domain_to_lexxi ? 1 : 0
  static_web_app_id = module.static_web_app_lexxi.static_web_app_id
  domain_name       = "lexxi-az-dev.ixxdna.com"
  validation_type   = "cname-delegation"
}



##=============================================================================
## STATIC WEB APP MODULE (rd)
##=============================================================================
## Azure Static Web App for Angular frontend deployment
## Features:
## - Free tier (no cost)
## - GitHub Actions CI/CD integration
## - Managed HTTPS certificate (*.azurestaticapps.net)
## - Global CDN distribution
## - API proxy to backend via staticwebapp.config.json
## 
## Deployment:
## - Automatically deployed from develop branch
## - Build command: ng build
## - Build output: dist/
## - GitHub Enterprise authentication via PAT token
##
## Access:
## - Frontend: https://<app-name>.azurestaticapps.net
## - API: Proxied through Static Web App to Front Door endpoint
##

module "static_web_app_rd" {

  source = "../../modules/static-web-app"

  resource_group_name = azurerm_resource_group.rg.name
  location            = var.azure_region
  environment         = var.environment
  resource_prefix     = local.resource_prefix
  app_name            = "rd"

  # GitHub Configuration
  github_repo_url = var.rd_github_repo_url
  github_token    = var.github_token
  github_branch   = var.rd_github_branch

  # Build Configuration
  app_location    = var.swa_app_location
  output_location = var.swa_output_location
  build_command   = var.swa_build_command

  # API Configuration
  api_endpoint = module.front_door.endpoint_fqdn
  api_key      = ""

  sku_tier = "Free"
  sku_size = "Free"
  tags     = local.tags

  depends_on = [
    module.front_door,
    azurerm_container_app.rd
  ]
}

## STATIC WEB APP CUSTOM DOMAIN for rd
resource "azurerm_static_web_app_custom_domain" "rd_dev" {

  count             = var.enable_custom_domain_to_rd ? 1 : 0
  static_web_app_id = module.static_web_app_rd.static_web_app_id
  domain_name       = "data-cleansing-az-dev.ixxdna.com"
  validation_type   = "cname-delegation"
}

## STATIC WEB APP CUSTOM DOMAIN for rd
resource "azurerm_static_web_app_custom_domain" "rnd_dev" {

  count             = var.enable_custom_domain_to_rd ? 1 : 0
  static_web_app_id = module.static_web_app_rd.static_web_app_id
  domain_name       = "rnd-dev.ixxdna.com"
  validation_type   = "cname-delegation"
}

1→##
     2→## Dev Environment - Input Variables
     3→## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
     4→##
     5→## Purpose: Defines configurable parameters for the development environment
     6→## These variables can be overridden via:
     7→## - Command line: terraform apply -var="key=value"
     8→## - .tfvars files: terraform apply -var-file="dev.tfvars"
     9→## - Environment variables: TF_VAR_key=value
    10→##
    11→## Current defaults are optimized for development cost and simplicity
    12→##
    13→
    14→##=============================================================================
    15→## ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION
    16→##=============================================================================
    17→## Basic environment identifiers and cloud region settings
    18→##
    19→
    20→variable "environment" {
    21→
    22→  description = "Environment name (dev/staging/prod)"
    23→  type        = string
    24→  default     = "dev"
    25→}
    26→
    27→variable "azure_region" {
    28→
    29→  description = "Azure region for deployment (e.g., eastus2, westus2, eastus22)"
    30→  type        = string
    31→  default     = "eastus2"
    32→}
    33→
    34→
    35→
    36→##=============================================================================
    37→## ORGANIZATION IDENTIFIERS
    38→##=============================================================================
    39→## Used for resource naming, tagging, and resource group organization
    40→##
    41→
    42→variable "company_name" {
    43→
    44→  description = "Company name (used in resource naming)"
    45→  type        = string
    46→  default     = "intellxxdna"
    47→}
    48→
    49→variable "project_name" {
    50→
    51→  description = "Project name (used in resource naming)"
    52→  type        = string
    53→  default     = "lexxi"
    54→}
    55→
    56→
    57→
    58→##=============================================================================
    59→## NETWORK CONFIGURATION
    60→##=============================================================================
    61→## Virtual Network address space
    62→## Default: 10.0.0.0/16 provides 65,536 addresses
    63→## Subnets defined in main.tf:
    64→## - Database: 10.0.2.0/24 (250 usable addresses)
    65→## - Container-Apps: 10.0.3.0/24 (250 usable addresses)
    66→## - Legacy-Apps: 10.0.4.0/24 (250 usable addresses)
    67→##
    68→
    69→variable "vnet_address_space" {
    70→
    71→  description = "VNet address space in CIDR notation"
    72→  type        = list(string)
    73→  default     = ["10.0.0.0/16"]
    74→}
    75→
    76→
    77→
    78→##=============================================================================
    79→## POSTGRESQL DATABASE CONFIGURATION
    80→##=============================================================================
    81→## PostgreSQL Flexible Server deployment settings
    82→## Only used in dev environment for private database access
    83→##
    84→
    85→variable "postgres_admin_username" {
    86→
    87→  description = "PostgreSQL administrator username (8-128 characters)"
    88→  type        = string
    89→  sensitive   = true
    90→}
    91→
    92→variable "intellxxdna_admin_username" {
    93→
    94→  description = "Intellxxdna sername (8-128 characters)"
    95→  type        = string
    96→  sensitive   = true
    97→}
    98→
    99→variable "postgres_admin_password" {
   100→
   101→  description = "PostgreSQL administrator password (8-128 characters, must include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, special chars)"
   102→  type        = string
   103→  sensitive   = true
   104→}
   105→
   106→variable "intellxxdna_admin_password" {
   107→
   108→  description = "Intellxxdna username password (8-128 characters, must include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, special chars)"
   109→  type        = string
   110→  sensitive   = true
   111→}
   112→
   113→
   114→
   115→##=============================================================================
   116→## CONTAINER APPS CONFIGURATION
   117→##=============================================================================
   118→## Shared Container Registry and Container Apps deployment settings
   119→##
   120→
   121→variable "shared_container_registry_login_server" {
   122→
   123→  description = "Shared ACR login server (e.g., ixdnaacrshared.azurecr.io)"
   124→  type        = string
   125→}
   126→
   127→variable "shared_container_registry_username" {
   128→
   129→  description = "ACR username for pulling images"
   130→  type        = string
   131→  sensitive   = true
   132→}
   133→
   134→variable "shared_container_registry_password" {
   135→
   136→  description = "ACR password for pulling images"
   137→  type        = string
   138→  sensitive   = true
   139→}
   140→
   141→variable "container_rd_app_image_name" {
   142→
   143→  description = "Container App image name (e.g., rd)"
   144→  type        = string
   145→  default     = "rd"
   146→}
   147→
   148→variable "container_lexxi_app_image_name" {
   149→
   150→  description = "Container App image name (e.g., lexxi)"
   151→  type        = string
   152→  default     = "lexxi"
   153→}
   154→
   155→variable "container_app_cpu" {
   156→
   157→  description = "Container App CPU allocation (e.g., 1.0)"
   158→  type        = string
   159→  default     = "1.0"
   160→}
   161→
   162→variable "container_app_memory" {
   163→
   164→  description = "Container App memory allocation (e.g., 2Gi)"
   165→  type        = string
   166→  default     = "2Gi"
   167→}
   168→
   169→variable "container_app_min_replicas" {
   170→
   171→  description = "Minimum number of replicas"
   172→  type        = number
   173→  default     = 1
   174→}
   175→
   176→variable "container_app_max_replicas" {
   177→
   178→  description = "Maximum number of replicas"
   179→  type        = number
   180→  default     = 1
   181→}
   182→
   183→
   184→
   185→##=============================================================================
   186→## APPLICATION GATEWAY CONFIGURATION
   187→##=============================================================================
   188→## Azure Front Door Configuration
   189→## Public-facing CDN with managed HTTPS and WAF for secure global access
   190→##
   191→
   192→variable "front_door_sku" {
   193→
   194→  description = "Front Door SKU: Standard_AzureFrontDoor (~$6/month) or Premium_AzureFrontDoor (~$25/month)"
   195→  type        = string
   196→  default     = "Standard_AzureFrontDoor"
   197→
   198→  validation {
   199→    condition     = contains(["Standard_AzureFrontDoor", "Premium_AzureFrontDoor"], var.front_door_sku)
   200→    error_message = "Front Door SKU must be Standard_AzureFrontDoor or Premium_AzureFrontDoor."
   201→  }
   202→}
   203→
   204→variable "enable_front_door_waf" {
   205→
   206→  description = "Enable Web Application Firewall on Front Door"
   207→  type        = bool
   208→  default     = true
   209→}
   210→
   211→variable "front_door_waf_mode" {
   212→
   213→  description = "WAF mode: Detection (log threats) or Prevention (block threats)"
   214→  type        = string
   215→  default     = "Detection"
   216→
   217→  validation {
   218→    condition     = contains(["Detection", "Prevention"], var.front_door_waf_mode)
   219→    error_message = "WAF mode must be Detection or Prevention."
   220→  }
   221→}
   222→
   223→
   224→
   225→##=============================================================================
   226→## STATIC WEB APP CONFIGURATION
   227→##=============================================================================
   228→## Azure Static Web App for Angular frontend deployment
   229→## Provides GitHub Actions CI/CD integration, managed HTTPS, and global CDN
   230→##
   231→
   232→variable "enable_custom_domain_to_lexxi" {
   233→
   234→  description = "Set to true only AFTER the CNAME record is created in DNS pointing to the default_host_name"
   235→  type        = bool
   236→  default     = false
   237→}
   238→
   239→variable "enable_custom_domain_to_rd" {
   240→
   241→  description = "Set to true only AFTER the CNAME record is created in DNS pointing to the default_host_name"
   242→  type        = bool
   243→  default     = false
   244→}
   245→
   246→
   247→variable "lexxi_github_repo_url" {
   248→
   249→  description = "GitHub repository URL (supports GitHub Enterprise URLs)"
   250→  type        = string
   251→
   252→  validation {
   253→    condition     = can(regex("^https://.*\\.com/.*", var.lexxi_github_repo_url))
   254→    error_message = "GitHub repository URL must be a valid HTTPS URL."
   255→  }
   256→}
   257→
   258→variable "rd_github_repo_url" {
   259→
   260→  description = "GitHub repository URL (supports GitHub Enterprise URLs)"
   261→  type        = string
   262→
   263→  validation {
   264→    condition     = can(regex("^https://.*\\.com/.*", var.rd_github_repo_url))
   265→    error_message = "GitHub repository URL must be a valid HTTPS URL."
   266→  }
   267→}
   268→
   269→variable "github_token" {
   270→
   271→  description = "GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) for authentication"
   272→  type        = string
   273→  sensitive   = true
   274→
   275→  validation {
   276→    condition     = length(var.github_token) > 0
   277→    error_message = "GitHub token cannot be empty."
   278→  }
   279→}
   280→
   281→variable "lexxi_github_branch" {
   282→
   283→  description = "GitHub branch to deploy from"
   284→  type        = string
   285→  default     = "develop"
   286→}
   287→
   288→variable "rd_github_branch" {
   289→
   290→  description = "GitHub branch to deploy from"
   291→  type        = string
   292→  default     = "develop"

content.content:
1→##
     2→## Storage Module - Main Configuration
     3→## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
     4→##
     5→## Purpose: Manages cloud storage, secrets, and logging infrastructure
     6→## Resources created:
     7→## - Storage Account: General-purpose v2, scalable cloud storage
     8→## - Key Vault: Secure secret and credential management
     9→## - Log Analytics Workspace: Centralized logging and monitoring
    10→##
    11→## Security features:
    12→## - HTTPS-only traffic (TLS 1.2 minimum)
    13→## - Blob versioning and soft delete for protection
    14→## - Key Vault access policies and RBAC
    15→## - OAuth-based authentication preferred
    16→##
    17→
    18→##=============================================================================
    19→## STORAGE ACCOUNT
    20→##=============================================================================
    21→## General-purpose v2 storage for application data, backups, and logs
    22→## Replication strategy varies by environment:
    23→## - Production: GZRS (geo-zone redundant)
    24→## - Dev/Staging: LRS (locally redundant)
    25→
    26→# Storage Account for application data
    27→resource "azurerm_storage_account" "sa" {
    28→  name                     = "ixdna${var.environment}asa${substr(replace(var.resource_group_name, "/[^a-z0-9]/", ""), 0, 6)}"
    29→  resource_group_name      = var.resource_group_name
    30→  location                 = var.location
    31→  account_tier             = "Standard"
    32→  account_replication_type = var.environment == "prod" ? "GZRS" : "LRS"
    33→
    34→  min_tls_version = "TLS1_2"
    35→
    36→  https_traffic_only_enabled      = true
    37→  shared_access_key_enabled       = true
    38→  default_to_oauth_authentication = true
    39→
    40→  blob_properties {
    41→    change_feed_enabled = var.environment == "prod"
    42→    versioning_enabled  = var.enable_backup
    43→    delete_retention_policy {
    44→      days = var.backup_retention_days
    45→    }
    46→  }
    47→
    48→  tags = var.tags
    49→}
    50→
    51→# Storage container for backups
    52→resource "azurerm_storage_container" "backup" {
    53→  name                  = "backups"
    54→  storage_account_name  = azurerm_storage_account.sa.name
    55→  container_access_type = "private"
    56→}
    57→

    76→# Key Vault for secrets management

##
## Storage Module - Main Configuration
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
##
## Purpose: Manages cloud storage, secrets, and logging infrastructure
## Resources created:
## - Storage Account: General-purpose v2, scalable cloud storage
## - Key Vault: Secure secret and credential management
## - Log Analytics Workspace: Centralized logging and monitoring
##
## Security features:
## - HTTPS-only traffic (TLS 1.2 minimum)
## - Blob versioning and soft delete for protection
## - Key Vault access policies and RBAC
## - OAuth-based authentication preferred
##

##=============================================================================
## STORAGE ACCOUNT
##=============================================================================
## General-purpose v2 storage for application data, backups, and logs
## Replication strategy varies by environment:
## - Production: GZRS (geo-zone redundant)
## - Dev/Staging: LRS (locally redundant)

# Storage Account for application data
resource "azurerm_storage_account" "sa" {
  name                     = "ixdna${var.environment}asa${substr(replace(var.resource_group_name, "/[^a-z0-9]/", ""), 0, 6)}"
  resource_group_name      = var.resource_group_name
  location                 = var.location
  account_tier             = "Standard"
  account_replication_type = var.environment == "prod" ? "GZRS" : "LRS"

  min_tls_version = "TLS1_2"

  https_traffic_only_enabled      = true
  shared_access_key_enabled       = true
  default_to_oauth_authentication = true

  blob_properties {
    change_feed_enabled = var.environment == "prod"
    versioning_enabled  = var.enable_backup
    delete_retention_policy {
      days = var.backup_retention_days
    }
  }

  tags = var.tags
}

# Storage container for backups
resource "azurerm_storage_container" "backup" {
  name                  = "backups"
  storage_account_name  = azurerm_storage_account.sa.name
  container_access_type = "private"
}

# Storage container for logs
resource "azurerm_storage_container" "logs" {
  name                  = "logs"
  storage_account_name  = azurerm_storage_account.sa.name
  container_access_type = "private"
}

##=============================================================================
## AZURE KEY VAULT
##=============================================================================
## Secure storage for secrets, keys, and certificates
## Access controlled through access policies and RBAC
## Used for storing:
## - Database credentials
## - API keys and tokens
## - Connection strings
## - SSH keys and certificates

# Key Vault for secrets management
resource "azurerm_key_vault" "kv" {
  name                = var.environment == "prod" ? "ixxdna-vault-${var.environment}-kv" : "ixdna-vault-${var.environment}-kv"
  location            = var.location
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  tenant_id           = data.azurerm_client_config.current.tenant_id
  sku_name            = "standard"

  soft_delete_retention_days = 7
  purge_protection_enabled   = var.environment == "production"

  access_policy {
    tenant_id = data.azurerm_client_config.current.tenant_id
    object_id = data.azurerm_client_config.current.object_id

    secret_permissions = [
      "Backup",
      "Delete",
      "Get",
      "List",
      "Purge",
      "Recover",
      "Restore",
      "Set",
    ]
  }

  tags = var.tags
}

# Log Analytics Workspace for monitoring
resource "azurerm_log_analytics_workspace" "law" {
  name                = "${var.resource_prefix}-law"
  location            = var.location
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  sku                 = "PerGB2018"
  retention_in_days   = var.log_retention_days

  tags = var.tags
}

# Data source for current Azure context
data "azurerm_client_config" "current" {}

file.content:
##
## Azure Front Door Module - Main Configuration
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
##
## Purpose: Creates Azure Front Door Standard with managed HTTPS for dev
## Configuration:
## - SKU: Standard (cost-optimized, ~$6/month)
## - HTTPS: Azure-managed (free, auto-renewal)
## - WAF: Enabled in Detection mode
## - Backend: Container App (internal FQDN)
##
## Features:
## - Global anycast CDN
## - Built-in DDoS protection
## - Managed certificate (*.azurefd.net domain)
## - Health probes & auto-failover
##

##=============================================================================
## WEB APPLICATION FIREWALL POLICY (FOR FRONT DOOR)
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_firewall_policy" "waf_policy" {

  count               = var.enable_waf ? 1 : 0
  name                = "waf${var.environment}fd"
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  sku_name            = "Standard_AzureFrontDoor"
  tags                = var.tags

  # Policy mode: Detection (logs) or Prevention (blocks)
  mode = var.waf_mode

  # Managed rule set only supported with Premium SKU
  # For Standard SKU, WAF uses default DDoS and basic protection
  dynamic "managed_rule" {
    for_each = var.front_door_sku == "Premium_AzureFrontDoor" ? [1] : []
    content {
      type    = "DefaultRuleSet"
      version = "1.0"
      action  = var.waf_mode == "Prevention" ? "Block" : "Log"
    }
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## FRONT DOOR PROFILE
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile" "fd_profile" {

  name                = "${var.resource_prefix}-fd"
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  tags                = var.tags
  sku_name            = var.front_door_sku

  response_timeout_seconds = 120
}

##=============================================================================
## FRONT DOOR ENDPOINT
##=============================================================================
## Public endpoint for accessing the service via CDN

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint" "fd_endpoint" {

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-fd-endpoint"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
  enabled                  = true
}

##=============================================================================
## ORIGIN GROUP (BACKEND POOL) - PER BACKEND WITH CUSTOM HEALTH PROBE
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group" "backend_group" {

  for_each = { for idx, backend in var.backends : backend.name => backend }

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-group"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
  session_affinity_enabled = each.value.session_affinity

  # Health probe configuration - customized per backend
  health_probe {
    interval_in_seconds = 60
    path                = each.value.health_path
    protocol            = "Http"
    request_type        = "GET"
  }

  # Load balancing settings
  load_balancing {
    sample_size                     = 4
    successful_samples_required     = 3
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## ORIGIN (BACKEND - MULTIPLE BACKENDS WITH DYNAMIC CREATION)
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin" "backend_origin" {

  for_each = { for idx, backend in var.backends : backend.name => backend }

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-origin"
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_group_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group.backend_group[each.value.name].id
  enabled                  = true

  host_name                       = each.value.fqdn
  http_port                       = 80  # Container Apps exposes on port 80, not the container target port
  https_port                      = 443
  origin_host_header              = each.value.fqdn
  certificate_name_check_enabled  = true
  priority                        = 1
  weight                          = 1000
}

##=============================================================================
## RULE SET FOR URL PREFIX STRIPPING
##=============================================================================

locals {
  backends_map = { for b in var.backends : b.name => b }

  # Backends that explicitly request path prefix stripping
  backends_with_strip = {
    for name, b in local.backends_map : name => b if b.strip_path_prefix == true
  }

  has_strip_backends = length(local.backends_with_strip) > 0
}

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set" "strip_prefix_rules" {
  count                    = local.has_strip_backends ? 1 : 0
  name                     = "${replace(var.resource_prefix, "-", "")}stripprefix"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
}

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule" "strip_path_prefix" {
  for_each = local.backends_with_strip

  name                      = "StripPrefix${title(each.value.name)}"
  cdn_frontdoor_rule_set_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.strip_prefix_rules[0].id
  # Order is alphabetical by name to ensure deterministic ordering
  order             = index(sort(keys(local.backends_with_strip)), each.key) + 1
  behavior_on_match = "Continue"

  conditions {
    url_path_condition {
      operator         = "BeginsWith"
      negate_condition = false
      # e.g. path="/rd/*" → match_values=["/rd/"]
      match_values = ["/${trimsuffix(trimprefix(each.value.path, "/"), "*")}"]
      transforms   = ["Lowercase"]
    }
  }

  actions {
    url_rewrite_action {
      # Strip the prefix: /rd/docs → /docs
      source_pattern          = "/${trimsuffix(trimprefix(each.value.path, "/"), "*")}"
      destination             = "/"
      preserve_unmatched_path = true
    }
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## ROUTES (PATH-BASED ROUTING TO DIFFERENT BACKENDS)
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_route" "backend_route" {

  for_each = local.backends_map

  name                          = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-route"
  cdn_frontdoor_endpoint_id     = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint.fd_endpoint.id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_group_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group.backend_group[each.value.name].id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_ids      = [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin.backend_origin[each.value.name].id]
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_path     = each.value.name == "keycloak" ? null : "/"
  cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain_ids = var.custom_domain_host_name != null ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain.custom_domain[0].id] : []
  cdn_frontdoor_rule_set_ids    = each.value.strip_path_prefix ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.strip_prefix_rules[0].id] : null
  enabled = true

  # Use HTTP for internal backends (unless specifically HTTPS)
  forwarding_protocol = each.value.protocol == "Https" ? "HttpsOnly" : "HttpOnly"
  patterns_to_match   = distinct([each.value.path, trimsuffix(each.value.path, "/*")])
  supported_protocols = ["Http", "Https"]
}

##=============================================================================
## CUSTOM DOMAIN
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain" "custom_domain" {
  count = var.custom_domain_host_name != null ? 1 : 0

  name                     = replace(replace(var.custom_domain_host_name, ".", "-"), "_", "-")
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
  host_name                = var.custom_domain_host_name

  tls {
    certificate_type    = "ManagedCertificate"
    minimum_tls_version = "TLS12"
  }
}


##=============================================================================
## WAF POLICY ASSOCIATION
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_security_policy" "waf_association" {

  count = var.enable_waf ? 1 : 0

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-waf-policy-assoc"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id

  security_policies {
    firewall {
      cdn_frontdoor_firewall_policy_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_firewall_policy.waf_policy[0].id

      association {
        domain {
          cdn_frontdoor_domain_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint.fd_endpoint.id
        }
        patterns_to_match = ["/*"]
      }
    }
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## CUSTOM DOMAIN (OPTIONAL - FOR PRODUCTION)
##=============================================================================
## Note: For production, you would configure a custom domain here
## For dev, we use the auto-generated Azure FD domain (*.azurefd.net)
