toolUseResult.originalFile:
##
## Azure Front Door Module - Main Configuration
## Author: Raul Peña (rpena@shokworks.io)
##
## Purpose: Creates Azure Front Door Standard with managed HTTPS for dev
## Configuration:
## - SKU: Standard (cost-optimized, ~$6/month)
## - HTTPS: Azure-managed (free, auto-renewal)
## - WAF: Enabled in Detection mode
## - Backend: Container App (internal FQDN)
##
## Features:
## - Global anycast CDN
## - Built-in DDoS protection
## - Managed certificate (*.azurefd.net domain)
## - Health probes & auto-failover
##

##=============================================================================
## WEB APPLICATION FIREWALL POLICY (FOR FRONT DOOR)
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_firewall_policy" "waf_policy" {

  count               = var.enable_waf ? 1 : 0
  name                = "waf${var.environment}fd"
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  sku_name            = var.front_door_sku
  tags                = var.tags

  # Policy mode: Detection (logs) or Prevention (blocks)
  mode = var.waf_mode

  # Managed rule set only supported with Premium SKU
  # For Standard SKU, WAF uses default DDoS and basic protection
  dynamic "managed_rule" {
    for_each = var.front_door_sku == "Premium_AzureFrontDoor" ? [1] : []
    content {
      type    = "DefaultRuleSet"
      version = "1.0"
      action  = var.waf_mode == "Prevention" ? "Block" : "Log"
    }
  }

}

##=============================================================================
## FRONT DOOR PROFILE
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile" "fd_profile" {

  name                = "${var.resource_prefix}-fd"
  resource_group_name = var.resource_group_name
  tags                = var.tags
  sku_name            = var.front_door_sku

  response_timeout_seconds = 120
}

##=============================================================================
## FRONT DOOR ENDPOINT
##=============================================================================
## Public endpoint for accessing the service via CDN

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint" "fd_endpoint" {

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-fd-endpoint"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
  enabled                  = true
}

##=============================================================================
## ORIGIN GROUP (BACKEND POOL) - PER BACKEND WITH CUSTOM HEALTH PROBE
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group" "backend_group" {

  for_each = { for idx, backend in var.backends : backend.name => backend }

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-group"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
  session_affinity_enabled = each.value.session_affinity

  # Health probe configuration - customized per backend
  health_probe {
    interval_in_seconds = 60
    path                = each.value.health_path
    protocol            = "Http"
    request_type        = "GET"
  }

  # Load balancing settings
  load_balancing {
    sample_size                     = 4
    successful_samples_required     = 3
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## ORIGIN (BACKEND - MULTIPLE BACKENDS WITH DYNAMIC CREATION)
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin" "backend_origin" {

  for_each = { for idx, backend in var.backends : backend.name => backend }

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-origin"
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_group_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group.backend_group[each.value.name].id
  enabled                  = true

  host_name                       = each.value.fqdn
  http_port                       = 80  # Container Apps exposes on port 80, not the container target port
  https_port                      = 443
  origin_host_header              = each.value.fqdn
  certificate_name_check_enabled  = true
  priority                        = 1
  weight                          = 1000
}

##=============================================================================
## RULE SET FOR URL PREFIX STRIPPING
##=============================================================================

locals {
  backends_map = { for b in var.backends : b.name => b }

  # Backends that explicitly request path prefix stripping
  backends_with_strip = {
    for name, b in local.backends_map : name => b if b.strip_path_prefix == true
  }

  has_strip_backends = length(local.backends_with_strip) > 0
}

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set" "strip_prefix_rules" {
  count                    = local.has_strip_backends ? 1 : 0
  name                     = "${replace(var.resource_prefix, "-", "")}stripprefix"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
}

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule" "strip_path_prefix" {
  for_each = local.backends_with_strip

  name                      = "StripPrefix${title(each.value.name)}"
  cdn_frontdoor_rule_set_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.strip_prefix_rules[0].id
  # Order is alphabetical by name to ensure deterministic ordering
  order             = index(sort(keys(local.backends_with_strip)), each.key) + 1
  behavior_on_match = "Continue"

  conditions {
    url_path_condition {
      operator         = "BeginsWith"
      negate_condition = false
      # e.g. path="/rd/*" → match_values=["/rd/"]
      match_values = ["/${trimsuffix(trimprefix(each.value.path, "/"), "*")}"]
      transforms   = ["Lowercase"]
    }
  }

  actions {
    url_rewrite_action {
      # Strip the prefix: /rd/docs → /docs
      source_pattern          = "/${trimsuffix(trimprefix(each.value.path, "/"), "*")}"
      destination             = "/"
      preserve_unmatched_path = true
    }
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## ROUTES (PATH-BASED ROUTING TO DIFFERENT BACKENDS)
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_route" "backend_route" {

  for_each = local.backends_map

  name                          = "${var.resource_prefix}-${each.value.name}-route"
  cdn_frontdoor_endpoint_id     = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint.fd_endpoint.id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_group_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin_group.backend_group[each.value.name].id
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_ids      = [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin.backend_origin[each.value.name].id]
  cdn_frontdoor_origin_path     = each.value.name == "keycloak" ? null : "/"
  cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain_ids = var.custom_domain_host_name != null ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain.custom_domain[0].id] : []
  cdn_frontdoor_rule_set_ids    = each.value.strip_path_prefix ? [azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_rule_set.strip_prefix_rules[0].id] : null
  enabled = true

  # Use HTTP for internal backends (unless specifically HTTPS)
  forwarding_protocol = each.value.protocol == "Https" ? "HttpsOnly" : "HttpOnly"
  patterns_to_match   = distinct([each.value.path, trimsuffix(each.value.path, "/*")])
  supported_protocols = ["Http", "Https"]
}

##=============================================================================
## CUSTOM DOMAIN
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain" "custom_domain" {
  count = var.custom_domain_host_name != null ? 1 : 0

  name                     = replace(replace(var.custom_domain_host_name, ".", "-"), "_", "-")
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
  host_name                = var.custom_domain_host_name

  tls {
    certificate_type    = "ManagedCertificate"
    minimum_tls_version = "TLS12"
  }
}


##=============================================================================
## WAF POLICY ASSOCIATION
##=============================================================================

resource "azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_security_policy" "waf_association" {

  count = var.enable_waf ? 1 : 0

  name                     = "${var.resource_prefix}-waf-policy-assoc"
  cdn_frontdoor_profile_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id

  security_policies {
    firewall {
      cdn_frontdoor_firewall_policy_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_firewall_policy.waf_policy[0].id

      association {
        domain {
          cdn_frontdoor_domain_id = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_endpoint.fd_endpoint.id
        }

        patterns_to_match = ["/*"]
      }
    }
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## DIAGNOSTIC SETTINGS
##=============================================================================
## Enable WAF and access logs to Log Analytics for monitoring and troubleshooting

resource "azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting" "front_door_diagnostics" {

  count = var.log_analytics_workspace_id != null ? 1 : 0

  name                       = "${var.resource_prefix}-fd-diagnostics"
  target_resource_id         = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
  log_analytics_workspace_id = var.log_analytics_workspace_id

  enabled_log {
    category = "FrontDoorAccessLog"
  }

  enabled_log {
    category = "FrontDoorHealthProbeLog"
  }

  enabled_log {
    category = "FrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallLog"
  }

  metric {
    category = "AllMetrics"
  }
}

##=============================================================================
## CUSTOM DOMAIN (OPTIONAL - FOR PRODUCTION)
##=============================================================================
## Note: For production, you would configure a custom domain here
## For dev, we use the auto-generated Azure FD domain (*.azurefd.net)

lines: -  # Managed rule set only supported with Premium SKU
-  # For Standard SKU, WAF uses default DDoS and basic protection
+  # Managed rule sets — Premium SKU only
+  # DRS 2.1: Latest OWASP ruleset covering SQLi, XSS, RCE, LFI, RFI and more
+  # Bot Manager 1.0: Blocks known bad bots, scrapers and vulnerability scanners

content.thinking:
The user wants to implement Private Link for Container Apps. This is a significant change that involves:

1. Making Container Apps internal (not publicly accessible)
2. Using Azure Front Door Premium's Private Link feature to connect to the Container Apps

In Azure Front Door Premium, you can use Private Link to connect to origins that are not publicly accessible. For Azure Container Apps, this requires:

1. Setting `internal_load_balancer_enabled = true` on the Container Apps Environment
2. Configuring Private Link on the Front Door origins

However, this is quite complex. Let me think about what's involved:

For Azure Container Apps with Private Link via Front Door Premium:


The Container Apps Environment needs to be configured with an internal load balancer, and Front Door Premium will create a Private Endpoint to connect to it, which then needs to be approved. The key is that Front Door Premium can connect directly to the Container Apps Environment's internal load balancer through this Private Link setup. I'm realizing that changing `internal_load_balancer_enabled` to true would require destroying and recreating the entire Container Apps Environment, which would cause downtime for all the apps currently running there—that's not a viable option for the existing setup.

Looking at what's already in place, the Container Apps have IP security restrictions that only allow Front Door backend IPs through, which effectively achieves the same security isolation as Private Link would without the operational overhead. For a dev environment, this approach is probably sufficient and avoids the complexity and downtime of a full environment recreation. I need to be clear about what this actually entails—enabling the internal load balancer will force a recreation of the entire Container Apps Environment, which means downtime and all apps need to be recreated. Before I proceed with the Terraform configuration for the private link setup, I should verify that the `azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin` resource properly supports private link blocks for Container Apps and understand the approval workflow that comes after.

content.text:
Before implementing, I need to be transparent about what this involves:

**What needs to change:**
1. Container Apps Environment needs `internal_load_balancer_enabled = true` — this **requires destroying and recreating the entire environment** including all 4 Container Apps (lexxi, rd, keycloak, mlflow)
2. Front Door origins need to be reconfigured with Private Link blocks
3. After apply, Private Endpoint connections need to be **manually approved** in the portal
4. **Downtime** during recreation — all services will be unavailable for several minutes

**What you gain:**
- Container Apps are completely private — no public internet access at all
- Traffic can ONLY reach them through Front Door
- Even if someone knows the Container App FQDN, they cannot connect directly

**For dev environment:** The current IP restriction approach (only allowing Front Door IPs on each Container App) already provides similar protection at much lower cost and complexity.

Do you want to proceed knowing there will be downtime, or would you prefer to keep the current IP restriction approach for dev and save Private Link for a staging/prod implementation?

message.content: I have some question.. 1) Frontdoor has a customer domain called api-dev.ixxdna.com, is going to be affected ? because if is destroyed and recreated, I need to call the owner again becasu that domiain is hanlder for a trirdparty. 2) my application that are using web statis web have custom domain as well, saem cuestion did for frontdoor.

content.text:
Great questions. Let me answer both:

---

**1. Front Door custom domain `api-dev.ixxdna.com`**

The Front Door profile itself is **NOT being destroyed** — only the Container Apps Environment and the Container Apps inside it. The Front Door profile, endpoints, routes, and custom domain remain untouched. So **no action needed with the third-party DNS owner**.

---

**2. Static Web Apps custom domains**

Same answer — Static Web Apps are completely independent from Container Apps. They are **not affected** by this change. Their custom domains (`lexxi-az-dev.ixxdna.com`, `data-cleansing-az-dev.ixxdna.com`, `rnd-dev.ixxdna.com`) remain intact.

---

**What WILL be destroyed and recreated:**
- Container Apps Environment
- All 4 Container Apps (lexxi, rd, keycloak, mlflow)

**What will NOT be touched:**
- Front Door profile, endpoints, routes, custom domain
- Static Web Apps and their custom domains
- PostgreSQL database and data
- Storage Account and artifacts
- Key Vault secrets

---

The only impact is **a few minutes of API downtime** while the Container Apps are recreated. Do you want to proceed?

content.thinking:
The user wants to implement Private Link for Container Apps but exclude Keycloak. The problem is that all Container Apps share the same Container Apps Environment (`azurerm_container_app_environment`). If we change `internal_load_balancer_enabled = true` on the environment, ALL Container Apps in that environment will be affected, including Keycloak.

There's no way to have some Container Apps in a private environment and others in a public environment within the same environment resource. Each Container App Environment is either internal or external.

Options:
1. Create a new private Container Apps Environment for lexxi, rd, and mlflow, while keeping the existing public environment for Keycloak


2. Move Keycloak to a different environment after testing

To exclude Keycloak from Private Link, I'd need to create a separate internal environment for the other apps, migrate lexxi, rd, and mlflow there, then configure Front Door to use Private Link for the new environment while maintaining the existing public route for Keycloak.

This approach works but adds operational overhead with two environments and slightly higher costs. I should present the user with the realistic tradeoffs: either make the entire environment private with downtime for all apps including Keycloak, create a second environment to keep Keycloak public, or defer Private Link implementation for now.

content.text:
Unfortunately that's not directly possible with the current architecture. All 4 Container Apps (lexxi, rd, keycloak, mlflow) share the **same Container Apps Environment**. Changing `internal_load_balancer_enabled = true` affects the entire environment — you can't make it private for some apps and not others.

To exclude Keycloak, the only option would be to create a **second Container Apps Environment** — one private (for lexxi, rd, mlflow) and one public (for keycloak). That adds complexity and a small cost increase.

**Options:**

1. **Create two environments** — private for lexxi/rd/mlflow, existing public for keycloak. More complex but lets you exclude Keycloak.

2. **Include Keycloak** — all 4 apps get recreated together. Keycloak data is safe (PostgreSQL database untouched), it just needs a few minutes to come back up.

3. **Skip Private Link for now** — current IP restriction approach is already reasonably secure for dev. Implement Private Link when you're ready to touch Keycloak too.

Which do you prefer?

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Deploy an MLflow Tracking Server on the dev Azure environment using Terraform
   - Use existing infrastructure (Azure Container Apps, PostgreSQL, Azure Storage, Key Vault, Front Door)
   - Restrict access to `/mlflow/` to only IP `143.105.136.68` (spent 2+ days troubleshooting WAF)
   - Eventually switched to MLflow built-in basic auth (`--app-name basic-auth`) for access control
   - Upgrade Azure Front Door from Standard to Premium SKU
   - Improve WAF configuration with DRS 2.1 and Bot Protection managed rulesets
   - Add rate limiting on `/auth/*` (Keycloak) against brute force
   - Fix Keycloak Terraform drift (ignore manual portal changes)
   - Add MLflow environment variables: `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI`, `MLFLOW_USERNAME`, `MLFLOW_PASSWORD`, `MLFLOW_ENABLED`, `MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED`, `MLFLOW_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`, `GIT_PYTHON_REFRESH`
   - Add Front Door diagnostic logging to Log Analytics
   - Skip Private Link for Container Apps for now (option 3)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Azure Container Apps (consumption plan, VNet-integrated)
   - Azure Front Door Premium (CDN-based, path-based routing, WAF)
   - Azure WAF custom rules, managed rulesets (DRS 2.1, BotManagerRuleSet 1.0)
   - Azure Key Vault secrets management
   - Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
   - Azure Blob Storage (wasbs:// artifact store)
   - MLflow Tracking Server with basic auth (`--app-name basic-auth`, `mlflow[auth]`)
   - Terraform remote state in Azure Storage (lease lock issues)
   - Terraform `ignore_changes` lifecycle for drift prevention
   - Terraform `-target` for selective applies
   - Terraform `state push errored.tfstate` and `terraform import` for state recovery
   - Azure Front Door WAF: Detection vs Prevention mode
   - Azure Monitor Diagnostic Settings for WAF logs

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `environments/dev/main.tf`
     - Most heavily modified file throughout the conversation
     - Added complete MLflow section:
       - `azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server_database.mlflow_database` (database: `mlflow`)
       - `azurerm_storage_container.mlflow_artifacts` (blob container: `mlflow-artifacts`)
       - `data "azurerm_storage_account" "storage"` (to get primary_connection_string)
       - Key Vault secrets: `mlflow_backend_store_uri`, `mlflow_artifact_root`, `mlflow_storage_connection_string`, `mlflow_tracking_uri`, `mlflow_admin_username`, `mlflow_admin_password`, `mlflow_flask_secret_key`, `mlflow_enabled`, `mlflow_tracing_enabled`, `mlflow_allowed_origins`
       - `azurerm_container_app.mlflow` with basic auth startup command
       - `azurerm_key_vault_access_policy.mlflow`
     - MLflow Container App startup command:
       ```hcl
       command = [
         "/bin/sh", "-c",
         "pip install 'mlflow[auth]' && printf '[mlflow]\\ndefault_permission = NO_PERMISSIONS\\ndatabase_uri = sqlite:////tmp/auth.db\\nadmin_username = %s\\nadmin_password = %s\\n' \"$MLFLOW_ADMIN_USERNAME\" \"$MLFLOW_ADMIN_PASSWORD\" > /tmp/basic_auth.ini && MLFLOW_AUTH_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/basic_auth.ini mlflow server --app-name basic-auth --backend-store-uri $MLFLOW_BACKEND_STORE_URI --artifacts-destination $MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_ROOT --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5000 --allowed-hosts ${local.resource_prefix}-mlflow.${azurerm_container_app_environment.env.default_domain},api-dev.ixxdna.com --cors-allowed-origins https://api-dev.ixxdna.com"
       ]
       ```
     - Added MLflow to Front Door backends at `/mlflow/*` with `strip_path_prefix = true`
     - Added `log_analytics_workspace_id = module.storage.log_analytics_workspace_id` to Front Door module
     - Keycloak `lifecycle` block updated:
       ```hcl
       lifecycle {
         ignore_changes = [workload_profile_name, template, secret]
       }
       ```
     - Added Lexxi container app env vars for MLflow: `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI`, `MLFLOW_USERNAME`, `MLFLOW_PASSWORD`, `MLFLOW_ENABLED`, `MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED`
     - Removed all WAF IP restriction code for `/mlflow/` (waf_custom_rules block)
     - Removed `AllowMLflowAuthorizedIP` ip_security_restriction from MLflow Container App

   - `environments/dev/variables.tf`
     - Changed `front_door_sku` default from `Standard_AzureFrontDoor` to `Premium_AzureFrontDoor`
     - Changed `front_door_waf_mode` default from `Detection` to `Prevention`
     - Added MLflow variables:
       ```hcl
       variable "mlflow_tracking_uri" {
         description = "MLflow tracking server URI used by client applications"
         type        = string
         default     = "https://api-dev.ixxdna.com/mlflow/"
       }
       variable "mlflow_flask_secret_key" {
         description = "Flask secret key for CSRF protection in MLflow basic auth"
         type        = string
         sensitive   = true
         validation {
           condition     = length(var.mlflow_flask_secret_key) >= 16
           error_message = "Flask secret key must be at least 16 characters long."
         }
       }
       variable "mlflow_admin_username" { ... }
       variable "mlflow_admin_password" { ... }
       variable "mlflow_allowed_origins" { default = "https://api-dev.ixxdna.com" }
       variable "mlflow_enabled" { type = bool, default = true }
       variable "mlflow_tracing_enabled" { type = bool, default = true }
       ```

   - `modules/front-door/main.tf`
     - Fixed WAF policy `sku_name` from hardcoded `"Standard_AzureFrontDoor"` to `var.front_door_sku`
     - Upgraded managed ruleset from `DefaultRuleSet 1.0` to `Microsoft_DefaultRuleSet 2.1`
     - Added `Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet 1.0`
     - Added rate limiting custom rule for `/auth/*`:
       ```hcl
       custom_rule {
         name                           = "RateLimitKeycloak"
         enabled                        = true
         priority                       = 100
         type                           = "RateLimitRule"
         action                         = "Block"
         rate_limit_threshold           = 100
         rate_limit_duration_in_minutes = 1
         match_condition {
           match_variable = "RequestUri"
           operator       = "BeginsWith"
           match_values   = ["/auth/"]
         }
       }
       ```
     - Added diagnostic settings resource:
       ```hcl
       resource "azurerm_monitor_diagnostic_setting" "front_door_diagnostics" {
         count = var.log_analytics_workspace_id != null ? 1 : 0
         name                       = "${var.resource_prefix}-fd-diagnostics"
         target_resource_id         = azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_profile.fd_profile.id
         log_analytics_workspace_id = var.log_analytics_workspace_id
         enabled_log { category = "FrontDoorAccessLog" }
         enabled_log { category = "FrontDoorHealthProbeLog" }
         enabled_log { category = "FrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallLog" }
         metric { category = "AllMetrics" }
       }
       ```
     - WAF association: reverted custom domain addition (removed dynamic domain block for custom domain)
     - Removed `dynamic "custom_rule"` block (WAF custom rules for MLflow IP restriction)

   - `modules/front-door/variables.tf`
     - Added `log_analytics_workspace_id` variable (default null)
     - Added and then removed `waf_custom_rules` variable

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **WAF not blocking `/mlflow/`**: Spent 2+ days troubleshooting. Root causes found:
     - WAF was in Detection mode (not blocking) → switched to Prevention
     - WAF policy `sku_name` hardcoded to Standard → fixed to use `var.front_door_sku`
     - WAF not associated with custom domain `api-dev.ixxdna.com` → added dynamic domain block (later removed)
     - Standard SKU doesn't support `SocketAddr` IP matching → upgraded to Premium
     - `FrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallLog` not flowing → added diagnostic settings via Terraform
     - Ultimately abandoned WAF IP restriction in favor of MLflow basic auth
   - **MLflow basic auth `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'flask_wtf'`**: Fixed by adding `pip install 'mlflow[auth]'` to startup command
   - **MLflow `MLFLOW_FLASK_SERVER_SECRET_KEY` required**: Added new Key Vault secret and env var
   - **`MLFLOW_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` pointing to wrong secret**: Was referencing `mlflow_flask_secret_key` instead of `mlflow_allowed_origins` → fixed
   - **Terraform state lease errors (412)**: Recurring issue. Fixed repeatedly with:
     ```bash
     az storage blob lease break --blob-name "dev/terraform.tfstate" --container-name "terraform-state" --account-name "tfstate29066"
     terraform state push errored.tfstate
     ```
   - **Key Vault secrets already exist**: Fixed with `terraform import` for each secret
   - **Keycloak Terraform drift**: Manual changes (probes, volume mounts, `KC_TRUSTSTORE_PATHS`, secrets) detected by Terraform → fixed with `ignore_changes = [workload_profile_name, template, secret]`
   - **Front Door managed_rule error on Standard SKU**: `managed_rule` field only supported on Premium → fixed by changing SKU to Premium
   - **MLflow env vars not appearing in container**: State out of sync → used `terraform apply -replace=azurerm_container_app.mlflow`

5. Problem Solving:
   - MLflow IP restriction via WAF was abandoned after extensive troubleshooting (2+ days). Basic auth via MLflow's built-in `--app-name basic-auth` was implemented instead, which is application-level and guaranteed to work regardless of Azure infrastructure
   - Terraform state lease lock recurring issue resolved each time by breaking the blob lease and pushing errored state
   - Front Door Premium upgrade unblocked WAF custom rules and managed rulesets
   - Keycloak drift prevention implemented via `ignore_changes` to avoid reverting manual portal changes
